notes.note: A jocular periphrasis: "the son of Amyntas" is Philip II of Macedon, and Cicero uses it to mean a tediously talkative neighbour named Philippus who broke in on his solitude at Astura. The Greek tag that follows complains of his "unending, tedious chatter."

Cicero is writing from his seaside retreat at Astura, on an islet at the mouth of the river Astura south of Antium, where he withdrew to grieve after Tullia's death in 45 BC. The villa, shore, and sea-view he praises here are Astura's.
